questions.prompt: Why does React Testing Library push you toward `getByRole` instead of `getByTestId`?

choices.text: getByRole queries the DOM the way assistive tech (screen readers, keyboard navigation) does — matches actual user paths. It naturally pushes you toward accessible components: if you can't find a button by role, the button isn't accessible. data-testid is an escape hatch for cases where no semantic option works; relying on it for everything creates tests that pass while users with disabilities can't use the feature. The query priority (role → label → placeholder → text → display value → alt text → title → test ID) reflects how visible/important each path is to real users.

questions.explanation: getByRole matches how assistive tech navigates — pushes toward accessible components.

questions.prompt: Component fetches data from `/api/posts`. How to test it without hitting real network?

choices.text: Use MSW (Mock Service Worker). Define handlers like `http.get('/api/posts', () => HttpResponse.json([...]))` and start a test server in beforeAll. The component's fetch calls go out as normal — but MSW intercepts at the network layer and returns mocked responses. Cleaner than fetch-mocking because your component code stays unchanged (no test-only branches), and the mock is closer to a real API. Reset handlers between tests for isolation. Works for fetch, axios, any HTTP library. Becoming the standard pattern for testing components that talk to APIs.

questions.explanation: MSW intercepts at network layer; component code unchanged; cleaner than fetch mocks.
